2000-11-01.title: Grieving Relatives Seek Kin Among Taiwan Crash Victims
2000-11-01.abstract: LOS ANGELES -- David Ralph's family got the telephone call they feared most.

2000-11-01.title: Americans Find Their Beachfront Dreams Washed Out in Mexico
2000-11-01.abstract: Mexican police continue to evict over 200 retirees, most of them Americans, from their homes in Baja Beach & Tennis Club, Ensenada, Baja California; Mexico's Supreme Court, which ruled in 1996 that land leased to resident was illegally seized by government in 1970's, has newly ordered government to carry out its original finding; many residents seek to negotiate leases with new owners of land (M)

2000-11-01.title: A Survivor´s Relative: ´We Pray for all the Other Families´
2000-11-01.abstract: LOS ANGELES -- David Ralph's family got the telephone call they feared most.

2000-11-01.title: Israel Agrees With Palestinians to Halt Violence
2000-11-01.abstract: JERUSALEM, Nov. 1 — In a chaotic and contradictory day, Israelis and Palestinians renewed high-level diplomatic contacts despite deadly gun battles and Israeli helicopter attacks on sites in the West Bank.

2000-11-01.title: New Violence After Rocket Strikes on Palestinians
2000-11-01.abstract: Israel's helicopter gunship strikes against Al Fatah offices in Ramallah and Nablus and headquarters of Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat's elite security guard in Gaza draw spur more defiance and clashes, adding five more Palestinians to death toll (M)

2000-11-02.title: Kosovo Albanians Cheer the Return of a Doctor Freed in Serbia
2000-11-02.abstract: Flora Brovina, prominent doctor, poet and activist for Kosovo Albanians, is freed from Serbian prison by special pardon from new Yugoslav president, Vojislav Kostunica, and crosses into Kosovo, her home province; she had been arrested outside her Pristina apartment by Serbian police on Apr 20, 1999, during NATO bombing campaign; she was sentenced to 12 years in Serbian court on charges of conspiring to commit terrorism and aiding rebel force, Kosovo Liberation Army; her release indicates that Pres Kostunica regards issue of detainees who had been transferred from Kosovo when Serbian forces withdrew as one of human rights (M)

2000-11-02.title: Suit in Nuns' 1980 Deaths in El Salvador Goes to Florida Jury
2000-11-02.abstract: Federal jury will decide whether two former military commanders in El Salvador will be held accountable for kidnappings, rapes and murders of four American churchwomen there 20 years ago; women's relatives are seeking $25 million a victim in damages (M)

2000-11-02.title: Ralph Nader's High-Wire Politics
2000-11-02.abstract: Alasdair Denvil letter, commenting on Oct 31 article on Vice Pres Al Gore's populist appeals in Wisconsin, says it is pity he lacks forum in which to answer Ralph Nader's criticisms and win back some of support that has deserted him

2000-11-02.title: West Bank Tightens Its Belt, Defiantly
2000-11-02.abstract: About 120,000 laborers from West Bank and Gaza Strip are unable to reach their jobs in Israel since borders were shut by Israelis when Palestinian unrest erupted five weeks ago; their lost wages are estimated at nearly $3.5 million a day; border closings and isolation of Palestinian towns behind Israeli Army checkpoints are preventing imports of raw materials and goods, blocking exports and disrupting marketing; as new uprising takes mounting economic toll, Palestinians insist they are prepared to pay price as long as necessary to end Israeli occupation and win independence (M)

2000-11-02.title: Israeli Missiles Hit Jericho, Where Gamblers Used to Hit Jackpots
2000-11-02.abstract: Recent violence has sidetracked thriving tourist industry in Palestinian-controlled West Bank city of Jericho; it is surrounded by Israeli tanks and military roadblocks and littered by burnt tires and spent bullets, result of nightly rituals of sniping at Israeli soldiers and retaliation by Israeli tanks; photo (M)

2000-11-03.title: Car Bomb Kills 2 Israelis and Threatens Truce Effort
2000-11-03.abstract: Car bomb kills two Israelis after it explodes near open-air market in Jerusalem, threatening early morning truce agreement between Israel and Palestinians; bombers flee on foot; site of bomb has been favorite target of Islamic groups during previous waves of terrorism; despite cease-fire pact, Palestinian gunmen open fire once more on Gilo, southeastern Jerusalem neighborhood, and Israeli troops return machine-gun fire toward shooting in Palestinian town of Beit Jala; 17-year-old Palestinian is shot dead in clash in village of Hizme near Jerusalem; Prime Min Ehud Barak of Israel calls Yasir Arafat, Palestinian leader, to express consolences; death toll from monthlong conflict rises to 168; photos; map (M)

2000-11-03.title: World Briefing
2000-11-03.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: PUTIN OPENS DOOR TO PAST -- President Vladimir V. Putin has agreed to permit a search of Soviet archives for data on the millions who died or were jailed under Stalin's rule, a leader in the effort to rehabilitate Stalin's victims said. Aleksandr Yakovlev, a former adviser to Mikhail S. Gorbachev, said the president ''takes to heart this tragic period in our history,'' and had ordered military and intelligence agencies to open their archives to help clear the names of unfairly persecuted citizens.      Michael Wines (NYT)

2000-11-03.title: Arrests Spur French Fears Over Spread Of Mad Cow
2000-11-03.abstract: New concern about mad cow disease is set off in France by arrest of father and son who tried to sell diseased cow for slaughter and succeeded in getting hundreds of pounds of suspect meat onto supermarket shelves; government, prompted by insistent calls for stricter protective measures, says it will increase testing of cattle headed for slaughterhouses, including random testing of even healthy-looking cows; so far this year, French officials habe detected 78 cases, more than twice number detected in 1999 (M)

2000-11-03.title: On the Streets, Palestinians Vow to Keep Fight Going
2000-11-03.abstract: Palestinians in Ramallah in West Bank feel grim satisfaction viewing television images of dead and wounded Israelis in Jerusalem car bombing, after so much bloodshed on Palestinian side in recent weeks; they oppose cease-fire and doubt that agreement between Shimon Peres and Yasir Arafat will not work; Palestinians throughout West Bank and Gaza bitterly denounce any idea of easing up their monthlong intifada (M)

2000-11-03.title: Estrada Crisis Deepens but He Vows to Fight
2000-11-03.abstract: MANILA, Nov. 3 — The crisis surrounding Philippine President Joseph Estrada deepened on Friday as a number of key government allies deserted the embattled leader, leaving him almost certain to be impeached over a gambling pay-offs scandal.

2000-11-04.title: World Briefing
2000-11-04.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    IRAN: PROTEST CROWDS DIMINISH  With traditional chants of ''Death to America!'' Iranians marked the anniversary of the United States Embassy seizure in 1979, but in much smaller numbers than in previous years. The small size of the crowd -- about 6,000 instead of the tens of the thousands seen in the mid-1990's -- was seen as proof of a growing desire among Iranians for better ties with the outside world.    (AP)

2000-11-04.title: So Boson Arrives At the Party . . .
2000-11-04.abstract: Physics of Higgs boson is so hard to explain that scientists once competed in contest to see if anyone could do it in less than one page; excerpts from one entrant's explanation (S)

2000-11-04.title: Insular Congress Appears to Be Myth
2000-11-04.abstract: Vast majority of members of Congress hold passports and use them, traveling overseas at least twice a year on trips that are largely business with some pleasure; nearly one-third of them have studied or worked abroad, and one in five claims to speak foreign language will enough to conduct business beyond America's borders; these statistics seem to contradict popular notion that members of Congress are homebound and uninterested in world; but statistics cannot serve as indicator of how any member of Congress is likely to vote on issues of foreign or national security (M)

2000-11-04.title: Cape Town Journal; Bones in Museum Cases May Get Decent Burials
2000-11-04.abstract: South Africa's museums and universities will open their archives to relatives of 2,000 members of Khoisan people whose dead bodies were taken, over their families' protests, for examination by scientists in early 1900's; for museums, it is act of penance, frank condemnation of their feverish race to collect and classify bones of so-called 'primitive races' including Khoisans, known then as Bushmen and Hottentots; museum curators will also arrange for proper burials of bones, in hope of restoring dignity taken from people who were denigrated in life and in death; photo (M)

2000-11-04.title: Doctors Back Many Palestinian Accusations, but Not All
2000-11-04.abstract: Physicians for Human Rights, based in Boston, reports on its independent investigation into past month's violence in Israel; report echoes many Palestinian accusations against Israel, but Palestinian officials criticize study for finding that one well-publicized Palestinian death was result of traffic accident, rather than torture-death at hands of Israeli settlers and soldiers; report also includes detailed analysis of 31 deaths over 24 days in Gaza; 38 percent of deaths are of children younger than 18; group says half the fatal gunshots in Gaza cases it studied were to the head (M)

2000-11-05.title: Call for 'Guiding Culture' Rekindles Political Debate in Germany
2000-11-05.abstract: Friedrich Merz, leading conservative, ignites political storm in Germany by saying that millions of foreigners in country must adopt German 'Leitkultur,' or guiding culture; critics like Foreign Min Joschka Fischer see such ideas as inflammatory departure from Germany's postwar embrace of muted identity within unifying Europe (M)

2000-11-05.title: Remote Yemen May Be Key to Terrorist's Past and Future
2000-11-05.abstract: FBI agents investigating terrorist bombing of US destroyer Cole in port of Aden, Yemen, visit country's remote, and fundamentalist, Wadi Doan region in search for clues to character of Osama bin Laden, Saudi dissident who is prime suspect in bombing which killed 17 American sailors; father who was born in area migrated to Saudi Arabia in 1950's; bin Laden, who never lived in Wadi Doan, is 17th of more than 50 children born to his father; maps; photos (M)

2000-11-05.title: In Marking Slaying Of Rabin in 1995, Israel Seeks Lessons
2000-11-05.abstract: Israel marks fifth anniversary of assassination of Prime Min Yitzhak Rabin even as new crisis with Palestinians threatens his legacy as peacemaker; his daughter Dalia Rabin-Pelosoff, Oslo negotiator Uri Savir and those at memorial rally mourn in heavy mood, while at Labor Party memorial service the famous photo of Rabin's handshake with Yasir Arafat is absent from elegiac slide show; photos; Rabin's widow is hospitalized, gravely ill with lung cancer (M)

2000-11-05.title: Mideast Unrest Stalls Israel's Role in Red Cross
2000-11-05.abstract: Violence in Mideast leads Switzerland to hastily postpone diplomatic conference to pave way for Israel's Magen David Adom to join Red Cross; 189 nations that have signed Geneva Conventions need to approve neutral emblem alongside red cross and red crescent; traditional cross was not intended as religious symbol but as honor to Swiss who helped found organization in mid-1800's (S)

2000-11-05.title: Runaway Youths a Thorn in Iran's Chaste Side
2000-11-05.abstract: Runaway youths, male and female, in shelters and public places in Iran are embarrassing testimony to failure of 21-year-old Islamic revolution to produce new generation of obedient, chaste 'warriors for Islam'; government ignored problem for years but is forced into action as more and more children take to streets, and fall prey to prostitution, crime and heroin addiction; city of Tehran now finances shelter for girls fleeing abusive homes; photo (M)

2000-11-06.title: C.I.A. Links Cited on Peru Arms Deal That Backfired
2000-11-06.abstract: Fifty thousand surplus assault rifles that were sold by Jordan to Peruvian military in 1998 with CIA's approval end up with Colombian guerrillas in deal involving ousted Peruvian intelligence chief, Vladimiro Montesinos, crucial CIA ally in region; disclosure raises new questions about Montesinos's ties to agency and has deepened rift in Clinton administration over how closely it should be dealing with official linked to human rights abuses and other crimes; Montesions has been implicated in arms deal by two of men who organized it since his ouster in September; Peruvian government is moving to prosecute him in connection with large personal fortune he apparently hid in Swiss banks; photo (M)

2000-11-06.title: Hopes Are Low for Clinton's Talks With Barak and Arafat
2000-11-06.abstract: White House says Prime Min Ehud Barak and Yasir Arafat will meet separately with Pres Clinton in coming days; expectations of success are low since Clinton, who is lame-duck president, may be unable to achieve much beyond trying to exercise calming influence (M)

2000-11-06.title: Dubious Mandates at Home For Players in Mideast Talks
2000-11-06.abstract: Prime Min Ehud Barak and Yasir Arafat are greatly weakened leaders who will try to revive moribund Israeli-Palestinian peace negotiations in coming series of meetings in Washington; both face sharp criticism from their own people; Palestinians are angry at Israel for still expanding settlements in West Bank and Gaza and controlling roads into Palestinian areas seven years after Oslo peace accord on which peace effort has been built; Israelis are disillusioned over recent outbreak of violence, called new intifada by Palestinians; many Israelis say violence proves they cannot live in peace with Palestinians; clashes between Palestinian protestors and Israeli troops continue; two Palestinians are shot and killed in Gaza; photo; map (M)

2000-11-06.title: Just Scratched by 1976 Arrest, Bush Keeps Lead in Polls
2000-11-06.abstract: WASHINGTON—  Governor George W. Bush on Sunday appeared to have survived, with only minor political scratches, the revelation last week of a past drunken driving conviction as he and Vice President Al Gore kept up vigorous last-minute efforts to energize party bases and assure crucial voter turnouts.

2000-11-06.title: 2 Religions Clashing in Ivory Coast
2000-11-06.abstract: Tensions are rising between Christians and Moslems in Ivory Coast, once envy of other African countries because of its religious and ethnic tolerance (M)

2000-11-07.title: New Clashes In West Bank And Gaza Put Israel on Edge
2000-11-07.abstract: Clashes between Palestinian protesters and Israeli forces continue in West Bank; two Palestinian youths are shot dead by troops; death toll since start of unrest on September 28 rises to 176; Israeli officials express concern over pattern of nightly gunfire from Palestinian areas toward Israeli settlements and other areas; say violence persists, despite truce agreement reached between Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat and Israeli elder statesman Shimon Peres; photo (M)

2000-11-07.title: Kumchon Journal; Railroad Trip to Europe? Koreans Savor the Idea
2000-11-07.abstract: Reestablishment of rail links between North and South Korea, cut off since start of Korean War 50 years ago, raises hopes among South Koreans who are stuck at end of peninsula at edge of Asian continent and feel almost as cut off from world geographically as Communist North has been isolated economically and politically; reconnection is intended to do away forever with notion of 'hermit kingdom' as Korea has been long known; maps; photos (M)

2000-11-07.title: Philippine House Panel Clears Impeachment of Estrada
2000-11-07.abstract: MANILA—  The impeachment complaint against President Joseph Estrada cleared the House Justice Committee in less than four hours Monday, and the stock market and Philippine currency soared in anticipation that he would resign or be removed.

2000-11-07.title: Nurse in Malawi Wages War on AIDS and Apathy
2000-11-07.abstract: Interview with Catherine Phiri, honored by UN for her anti-AIDS campaign in Malawi; she is founder of support group for people not only suffering from disease in country with one of highest infection rates in Africa, but also from neglect of society unschooled about malady; was diagnosed HIV-positive in 1990 after husband died from disease; photo (M)

2000-11-07.title: Markings Failed to Distinguish Fatal Runway : Pilots Have Long Faulted Taipei Airport's Hazard
2000-11-07.abstract: SINGAPORE—  For years pilots flying into Taiwan had complained about lighting and markings on the airstrip at Chaing Kai Shek Airport where a Singapore Airways jumbo jet crashed a week ago, killing or injuring all but 40 of the 179 people on board, according to two pilots familiar with the airport.

2000-11-08.title: Iraq Seeks Talks With U.N. Chief on Arms-Inspection Impasse
2000-11-08.abstract: Iraq asks for meeting with UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan in move to break impasse over arms inspections; Annansays he will meet with representatives of Iraqi government in summit-level meeting of Islamic nations in Qatar on November 11 (M)

2000-11-08.title: Chief U.S. Mediator in Mideast Plans to Leave in January
2000-11-08.abstract: Dennis B Ross, leading American peace negotiator in Middle East for more than decade, will leave post in January 2001, at end of Clinton administration; has served under administrations of Presidents Reagan, Bush and Clinton; cites stress on family from 12 years of travel as main reason for decision (M)

2000-11-08.title: Scandals Sully Indonesian Leader's Reform Image
2000-11-08.abstract: Criticism has grown year after Abdurrahman Wahid assumed power as Indonesia's president and pledged to end flagrant cronyism rampant under predecessor Pres Suharto; many say he has done little to change Indonesia's notoriously corrupt bureaucracy and legal system; public opinion poll finds 65 percent of those surveyed favor special legislative session to impeach him; critics are troubled that Wahid himself is now embroiled in embarrassing string of scandals that have damaged his credibility and hurt efforts to establish rule of law in young democracy; scandal involving his masseur, who is accused of bilking government of $4 million, discussed; photo (M)

2000-11-08.title: Siamese Twin Is Separated; 'Sadly Dies' To Save Sister
2000-11-08.abstract: Doctors in Manchester, England, separate 3-month-old twin girls in court-approved operation that knowingly sacrificed life of one to save other; surviving twin is in critical but stable condition following 20-hour operation; both were born with fused spines, joined at abdomen with arms and legs at right angles to their upper bodies (M)

2000-11-08.title: Margaret Atwood Is the Winner of Britain's Top Fiction Award
2000-11-08.abstract: Canadian writer Margarat Atwood wins 2000 Booker Prize, Britain's most prestigious literary award for fiction, for book The Blind Assassin; has been nominated three times before (M)

2000-11-09.title: Russian Official Says Evidence Points to Collision With the Sub
2000-11-09.abstract: Russia's deputy prime minister, Ilya Klebanov, says divers investigating cause of explosion that sank Russian submarine Kursk found 'serious visual evidence' pointing to collision between Kursk and another object; says investigative panel has still not ruled out other possible causes of disaster, which killed 118 sailors; senior Russian officials have said they believe Kursk collided with foreign submarine that was monitoring Russian naval exercises; American military officials have flatly denied that any of their boats came in contact with Kursk (M)

2000-11-09.title: A Night to Remember:Indecision 2000
2000-11-09.abstract: Events on Election Day in Florida (local time):

2000-11-09.title: Caracas Journal; Salutes, Some Skeptical, as Schools Go 'Bolivarian'
2000-11-09.abstract: Critics of Pres Hugo Chavez of Venezuela point to his new school system as most troubling sign of creeping militarization of government; new system offers three free meals and after-school care, under soldiers' supervision; in most cases, classes are still taught by civilians, and military is supervising schools; Constitution drawn up to Chavez's specifications last year makes teaching of 'Bolivarian ideology' obligatory in all schools; leading Bolivar scholars, educators and even retired generals are alarmed that it is Chavez and his government who define what is Bolivarian and what is not; photo (M)

2000-11-09.title: Coca Conflict in Colombia Snares the Innocent
2000-11-09.abstract: Putumayo, province of coca-growing jungle, is crucial battleground in Colombia's civil conflict; rebels have imposed travel ban there, strangling local economy and underscoring challenge that Pres Andres Pastrana faces as he works with American government in imposing $7.5 billion plan to curtail lucrative coca trade and weaken rebels; both rebels and paramilitaries appear firmly established and prepared to fight for months on end; photos; map (M)

2000-11-09.title: China Sentences 11 Officials to Death in Smuggling Scandal
2000-11-09.abstract: Chinese courts convict 84 people of corruption, smuggling and other charges, handing out 11 death sentences to government officials; these are first punishments meted out in connection with embarrassing multi-billion-dollar smuggling scandal in port city of Xiamen in Fujian Province; many of convicted officials are from Xiamen customs service; customs officials have recently been investigated in number of cities, including Tianjin and Shantou; Chinese experts say corruption may be equally widespread in other government bureaucracies (M)

2000-11-10.title: Israelis Track Down and Kill a Fatah Commander
2000-11-10.abstract: Israeli helicopter gunships fire missiles at vehicle carrying Palestinian paramilitary commander on West Bank, killing him and two women who happened to be nearby; this is Israel's first targeted, planned slaying of Palestinian militia leader since six-week-old violent confrontation on West Bank and Gaza began; Israeli Army spokesman says militia leader, Hussein Obaiyat, was suspected of being mastermind behind many shooting attacks on Israeli soldiers and civilians that emanated from Bethlehem area; says army received permission for killing from 'highest political level'; Fatah paramilitary field officer confirms that Obaiyat was commander of southern Bethlehem area; says Fatah will take 'escalatory measures and revenge' for his killing; paramilitary units of Fatah operate independently; Yasir Arafat's police and security services are supposed to disarm and restrain them under terms of many agreements with Israel; photos; map (M)

2000-11-10.title: Putin Cuts Forces by 600,000, Promising Military Overhaul
2000-11-10.abstract: Kremlin says it plans to shrink its three-million-strong armed forces by 600,000 people, led by deep cuts in main Defense Ministry forces; Pres Vladimor V Putin says he wants to rein in unwieldy and extravagant military machine; move may also presage deeper reshuffling of defense apparatus and more direct control of military by Putin (M)

2000-11-10.title: Corrections
2000-11-10.abstract: Correction of Nov 9 article on Brown University's selection of Ruth J Simmons as its president, regarding day of its decision and Simmons's age, which is 55

2000-11-10.title: Chidambaram Subramaniam, India's 'Green' Rebel, 90, Dies
2000-11-10.abstract: Chidambaram Subramaniam, political architect of the 'green revolution' in India, which made nation self-sufficient in wheat production, dies at 90; photo (M)

2000-11-10.title: Yalding Journal; England Is Half-Drowned and Wholly Fed Up
2000-11-10.abstract: Since mid-October, Britain has been pummeled by rain, whipped by winds and buffeted by storms that have caused untold millions of dollars in damage, wreaked havoc with roads and trains, and left thousands of people without habitable homes; at least 12 people have been killed, mostly from trees falling on their cars as they tried to drive through wind and rain; in Yalding, Kent, flooding has been so severe that firefighters swim down streets, cars float away, houses have been destroyed and residents have been evacuated by boat; photos; map (M)

2000-11-11.title: Argentina Says Lenders Pledge to Help Lift Economy
2000-11-11.abstract: Argentina Pres Fernando de la Rua says multinational credit agencies and international banks will back effort to revive stagnant economy, televised speech; outlines steps to help restore confidence; reiterates his support for Economy Min Jose Luis Machinea; analysts say plan could involve added credit line from International Monetary Fund and other lenders (M)/

2000-11-11.title: Bosnia Holds Broad Elections Today, and Western Officials Fret
2000-11-11.abstract: General elections are set for Bosnia; Western officials who run nation are closely watching vote; Wolfgang Petritsch, who heads international administration, has urged people to reject nationalist parties (M)

2000-11-11.title: An Endangered Species: Liberal in Canada's West
Lockerbie Prosecutors Work to Head Off Move to Shift Blame
2000-11-11.abstract: Prosecution tries to establish alibi for Mohamed Abo Talb, Palestinian defense plans to blame for bombing of Pan Am flight 103 over Lockerbie, Scotland; he testifies after delay of over month caused by still secret evidence concerning him after defense again tries to block testimony; serves life sentence for fatal Copenhagen bombing (M)/

2000-11-11.title: Eric Morley, 82, 'Miss World' Promoter, Dies
2000-11-11.abstract: Eric Morley, who organized and promoted Miss World contest and made parade of pretty girls in swimsuits into televised international sport with millions of fans, dies at 82; photo (M)

2000-11-12.title: John S. Morrison, Scholar, 87; Rebuilt a Lost Greek Warship
2000-11-12.abstract: Prof John S Morrison, scholar whose boyhood vision of ancient Greek boats known as triremes inspired modern re-creation of that juggernaut, dies at 87; photos (M)

2000-11-12.title: Hesitantly, Students in South Africa Reach Across the Racial Divide
2000-11-12.abstract: Number of black South African students commuting to white schools has soared in the five years since lifting of last vestiges of government-enforced segregation opened public schools to all races; more than third of all schools are said to be integrated; unprecedented mingling of black and white children has been overwhelmingly peaceful as black students savor first taste of quality education; photos (M)

2000-11-12.title: Cole Inquiry Provokes Bitter U.S. Dispute
2000-11-12.abstract: Bitter dispute erupts within Clinton administration over whether to accept Yemen's limits on American investigation of bombing of the destroyer Cole off Aden or press for wider-ranging inquiry that FBI officials believe could potentially lead to powerful people linked to Yemeni government itself; disagreement reportedly exists between FBI Dir Louis J Freeh and Amb Barbara K Bodine; photo (M)

2000-11-12.title: Bosnia Votes With Hope to Break Ethnic Parties' Rule
2000-11-12.abstract: Bosnians vote in general elections without excitement, with many expressing determination to try to bring change to country almost five years after signing of Dayton peace accord; polls suggest multiethnic Social Democratic Party, which has rejected nationalist politics and campaigned on trying to mend shattered economy, may gain power in several regions and become kingmaker on national level (S)

2000-11-12.title: Canada's 'Gentle Giants' Await Vanishing Winter
2000-11-12.abstract: Hudson Bay's polar bear population is fast becoming worldwide symbol of climate change as ice floes that are home to seal pups melt three weeks earlier than 25 years ago; bears have three weeks less each year to capture and gorge on pups; are 10 percent thinner and have 10 percent fewer cubs than they did 20 years ago; maps; photo (M)

2000-11-13.title: Suharto Son, Now a Fugitive, Flamboyantly Evades Capture
2000-11-13.abstract: Hutomo Mandala Putra, youngest son of Pres Suharto, who was sentenced to 18-month prison term in corruption case, vanishes after request for clemency from Indonesian Supreme Court is rejected; remained free following conviction through series of legal maneuvers and mistakes by prosecution that constitutional scholars describe as absurd; Indonesians say handling of case raises questions over government's commitment to establishing new rule of law after decades of corruption under Suharto's dictatorial rule (M)

2000-11-13.title: Foes, Poised for Impeachment, Plan a Strike : Rally Backing Estrada Attracts Over a Million
2000-11-13.abstract: MANILA—  As the Philippines braced for a stormy week of strikes and street protests, lawmakers said Sunday that President Joseph Estrada would very likely be impeached as early as Monday and his case sent to the Senate for trial.

2000-11-13.title: Exotic Gifts From Far Away:Holiday Shopping on the Road : JAKARTA (folo)
2000-11-13.abstract: For globe-trotting frequent travelers, the holidays can mean taking precious time out to shop in unfamiliar cities — but returning home laden with treasure. Here are suggestions for great gifts to pick up during your travels, compiled by our correspondents.

2000-11-13.title: European Nights:Dance the Dark Away : AMSTERDAM (folo)
2000-11-13.abstract: Whether you want a serious night on the town or a quiet drink in a congenial bar, Europe's club scene has it all.

2000-11-13.title: Clinton and Barak Meet With Reduced Expectations
2000-11-13.abstract: Pres Clinton and Prime Min Ehud Barak meet at White House to discuss ways to prevent further violence in West Bank and Gaza that has taken more than 200 lives over past six weeks; somber mood is far cry from heady mood 16 months ago, when Barak came as peacemaker willing to negotiate with Clinton and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat; photo (M)

2000-11-14.title: Walter McConaughy, 92, Envoy in Asia, Dies
2000-11-14.abstract: Walter Patrick McConaughy, specialist on Far East for State Department who had high-profile postings in Burma, Pakistan and Taiwan, dies at age 92; photo (M)

2000-11-14.title: World Briefing
2000-11-14.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: AMERICAN LOSES COURT ROUND -- A court rejected an appeal by an American accused of spying to call additional experts to determine whether documents about torpedoes that he received were top secret, as prosecutors allege. The American, Edmond Pope, was arrested in April and charged with trying illegally to obtain plans for the torpedo. He says the information had already been sold abroad.   (Associated Press)

2000-11-14.title: Putin Critic Yields Control of His Media Empire to the Kremlin
2000-11-14.abstract: Russian media mogul Vladimir V Gusinsky, fierce critic of government and deeply in debt, tentatively agrees to give up control of publishing and broadcasting empire to country's natural gas monopoly Gazprom, which is controlled by Russian government; agreement, subject to court review, raises questions about level of independence Gusinsky's company, Media-Most, will retain after gas company becomes its largest shareholder; Gusinksy is wanted for questioning by prosecutors in criminal case; left country in July after brief imprisonment for fraud, and warrant has been issued for arrest; he is perhaps single biggest target in campaign of legal and political pressure by Pres Vladimir Putin against prominent Russian businessmen who influenced politics in 1990's; is only one among Putin's opponents required to give up property; lawyers say he will not return to Russia to face questioning (M)

2000-11-14.title: The Day's Death Toll: Four Israelis and Two Palestinians
2000-11-14.abstract: Isra eli-Palestinian violence continues in West Bank and Gaza; Palestinian snipers kill two Israeli soldiers and civilian on West Bank highway and Palestinian gunman kills Israeli truck driver in Gaza; two Palestinian youths are fatally shot by Israeli soldiers; one-day toll is worst for Israel since start hostilities over six weeks ago; Prime Ehud Barak condemns attacks;; vows prompt and 'focused' military response; Israeli Army orders clampdown on movements in and out of Palestinian-governed areas; scours West Bank and Gaza for suspects in attacks; maps; photo (M)

2000-11-14.title: Nazi Labor Case Gains
2000-11-14.abstract: Newark, NJ, Federal Dist Judge William G Bassler dismisses 45 of the 49 lawsuits against German companies that used forced labor under Nazis; moves toward disbursing $5.1 billion already established in trust for victims (S)

2000-11-15.title: Clinton, in Brunei, Warns That Backtracking Could Bring a New Asian Economic Crisis
2000-11-15.abstract: Pres Clinton begins five-day farewell tour of Asia in Brunei, arguing that Asian Pacific Economic Cooperation group, which he began in 1993, has begun to weave region together for first time in history; warns that countries backtracking on economic reforms risk a return to the financial crisis of 1997; photo (M)

2000-11-15.title: Swiss Raise Sum in Bank Case, to $500 Million
2000-11-15.abstract: Swiss investigation turns up evidence that $500 million was transferred to Swiss banks from accounts at Bank of New York held by Becs, Benex International and Lowland, which were all opened by Peter Berlin as part of a Russian plot to avoid taxes and launder money; this compares with $16.8 million that was frozen in Swiss banks in 1999; opens possibility that Swiss authorities will require Swiss banks to conduct a new search of transactions with Bank of New York (M)

2000-11-15.title: Clinton Asks $750 Million in New Assistance for Middle East
2000-11-15.abstract: Pres Clinton asks Congress for an additional $750 million in emergency aid for Israel, Egypt and Jordan, drawing a skeptical reaction from some influential Republican lawmakers, particularly on the aid to Egypt (M)

2000-11-15.title: Should Israel Sacrifice Its Hopes for Peace for Settlers?
2000-11-15.abstract: Peace Now and other Israeli leftists, who were stunned into silence at first by the unraveling of Israeli-Palestinian relationship, are now raising their voices to argue that settlements in occupied territories are an obstacle to peace and a provocation for the present violence; some members of Four Mothers, grassroots organization that pushed for an Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon, are regrouping to advocate withdrawal from territories; settlers are outraged; photo (M)

2000-11-15.title: America Today, Through Vietnamese Eyes
2000-11-15.abstract: Vietnam today is fascinated with America: its freedom, its energy, its work ethic, its robust economy; America shimmers for the Vietnamese like a vision of their own potential success, even amid some doubts about America's freewheeling society and its overpowering role in the world and wartime memories that are still raw; admiration extends to Pres Bill Clinton, who arrives there on Nov 16, and Vietnamese goverment is at one with the people on that; photos (M)

2000-11-16.title: Case for a Serbian Democracy (Without Purges)
2000-11-16.abstract: Pres Vojislav Kostunica of Yugoslavia says rush to build new democracy cannot be done radically, without building on foundations of existing state, interview; he rejects arguments from his allies for rapid purge of top commanders of secret police and army; says he is eager for systemic transformation in police, army and judiciary, but this cannot occur before election of Parliament and very serious public debate; says he eagerly awaits early elections that have been called for Dec 23; country is relatively stable, but sharp debate rages within 18-party coalition about pace of change; photo (M)

2000-11-16.title: U.S. Study Points Out Ways to Cut Emissions
2000-11-16.abstract: Critical talks on treaty to fight global warming enter third day at The Hague; Dept of Energy issues study addressing main point of contention: whether United States could do more to reduce its addiction to fossil fuels; study finds inexpensive, fairly simple changes in American energy policies could go long way toward cutting emissions of carbon dioxide, dominant greenhouse gas; with 4 percent of world's population, US produces 25 percent of heat-trapping gases; US is feeling growing pressure to commit to reductions (M)

2000-11-16.title: Brazil's Chief, While Languishing, Is Challenged on Two Fronts
2000-11-16.abstract: Pres Fernando Henrique Cardoso of Brazil faces pair of new challenges: charges of campaign financing irregularities in his 1998 re-election campaign, and Brazilian Intelligence Agency spying on Luiz Francisco de Souza, federal prosecutor who uncovered campaign accounting irregularities (M)

2000-11-16.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-11-16.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: TORPEDO PLANS CALLED SECRET  A panel of experts told a Moscow court that the plans for a Russian Navy torpedo sought last April by Edmond Pope, a retired American intelligence agent charged with espionage, were in fact classified. That is at odds with arguments by Mr. Pope's lawyer that the material had largely been published elsewhere, and that its sale to Mr. Pope had been approved by the government.    Michael Wines (NYT)

2000-11-16.title: Mrs. Clinton Makes Plea for Peace at Rabin Funeral
2000-11-16.abstract: Hillary Rodham Clinton meets with president and prime minister of Israel and delivers emotional euology for Leah Rabin, who died Nov 11; Clinton makes plea to revive peace effort begun by Rabin's husband, Prime Min Yitzhak Rabin, who was assassinated five years ago; says Americans 'will always stand by Israel'; Prime Min Ehud Barak, in his brief eulogy, refers to Leah Rabin's 'tough criticism' of him for breakdown of peace talks; also attending service are German Pres Johannes Rau, Russian Foreign Min Igor Ivanov, and Dennis Ross, State Dept negotiator; conspicuously absent are dignitaries from Jordan or Egypt, or any high-level Palestinian officials; Yasir Arafat, who was invited, does not attend, but he expresses condolences in statement broadcast on Israeli television; also says he is renewing his commitment to peace; photo (M)

2000-11-17.title: World Briefing
2000-11-17.abstract: EUROPE    YUGOSLAVIA: MENDING TIES -- The new government moved to restore full diplomatic ties with the four countries that led the 1999 NATO bombing campaign: Britain, France, Germany and the United States. The announcement marked the latest move by President Vojislav Kostunica to speed up cooperation with the West after a decade of international isolation under Slobodan Milosevic.     (Agence France-Presse)

2000-11-17.title: CLINTON IN HANOI, INTENT ON FORGING NEW RELATIONSHIP
2000-11-17.abstract: Pres Clinton arrives in Vietnam, country fascinated by its former enemy and deeply ambivalent about opening its society and economy to world that America dominates; Clinton, who is greeted warmly by thousands of people lining streets of Hanoi, is first president to set foot in Vietnam since Richard M Nixon made unscheduled six-hour visit to troops outside Saigon in 1969; Clinton, who opposed war as young man, plans on this trip to focus resolutely on future; he will spend much of his visit celebrating results of his seven-year-long effort to draw Vietnam out of its self-imposed economic isolation, and his bet that prosperity in information age will necessitate measure of political openness and free flow of information there; many foreign companies that poured into Vietnam in early 1990's, after Clinton lifted trade embargo, have pulled up stakes in frustration over dealing with Vietnamese bureaucracy and corruption; photos (M)

2000-11-17.title: Resolute Commander of Israeli Forces in the West Bank
2000-11-17.abstract: Brig Gen Benjamin Gantz, commander of Israel's forces thoughout West Bank, holds that all along border, Palestinian gunmen have fired at houses and motorists in Israel, proving that their ultimate target is Israel itself, not simply settlements east of internationally recognized border; says Israel's unilateral withdrawal from West Bank is not an option; says it would trigger massacre, causing calamity for Palestinians who would be pulled into violence, and political disaster for Israel; Gantz dismisses negotiations aimed at having Palestinian and Israeli forces control violence cooperatively; says resulting contacts with his Palestinian counterparts had little effect; Gantz says he is steeled for low-intensity but long-term warfare; map (M)

2000-11-17.title: Russia Sounds Death Knell For Mir, Hardy Orbiter
2000-11-17.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin's cabinet issues go-ahead for proposal to crash Mir space station into Pacific Ocean in February; Yuri N Koptev, general director of Russian Space Agency, says 130-ton ship is corroding and leaking and that major components could fail at any time; Mir has been in orbit for almost 15 years (M)

2000-11-17.title: Arafat Differs With Barak On Chances Of Peace Deal
2000-11-17.abstract: Palestinian and Israeli leaders voice opposite assessments about chance of peace deal soon as American envoy, Dennis Ross, tries to prod them back to negotiating table after seven weeks of violence; Prime Min Ehud Barak of Israel says there could be no resumption of talks without significant reduction of Palestinian violence; Yasir Arafat, Palestinian leader, says he is hopeful that agreement could be reached before Pres Clinton leaves White House in January; photo (M)

2000-11-18.title: Annan Plans Mideast Talks on U.N. Force
2000-11-18.abstract: Sec Gen Kofi Annan will begin talks with Israelis and Palestinians about what kind of an international monitoring mission, if any, should be sent to West Bank and Gaza (S)

2000-11-18.title: Russian Tycoon Reports a Second Accord to Control Media
2000-11-18.abstract: Media-Most head Vladimir V Gusinsky reaches new accord aimed at reducing debt and preventing Russian government from gaining majority control of NTV, nation's leading independent television station; Gusinsky, deeply in debt to state-controlled Gazprom, agrees to let his interest in NTV fall, initially to 49 percent from 70 percent; pledges more shares to cover debts due next year and ultimately may be left with 25 percent stake in station; Gazprom will have 46 percent stake and Capital Research Management will retain 4 percent; accord does not need court approval, apparently putting it out of reach of federal prosecutors (M)

2000-11-18.title: Briton Is Killed in Car Blast Laid to Terrorists in Riyadh
2000-11-18.abstract: A British engineer was killed and his wife was wounded today when their car blew up on a busy commercial street here.     The explosion, apparently caused by a bomb or other device inside the four-wheel-drive vehicle, shattered the tense calm that has prevailed in the Persian Gulf since a terrorist attack on an American warship last month outside the port of Aden, in neighboring Yemen.

2000-11-18.title: Anversa Degli Abruzzi Journal; Adopt a Sheep, for Friend or Dinner Companion
2000-11-18.abstract: Manuela Cozzi's cooperative farm in Anversa degli Abruzzi, Italy, creates an online sheep adoption program to help keep region's sheep-raising traditions alive; $154 contract entitles adoptive 'parents' to a year's supply of their chosen one's merino wool, fresh cheese or, if they wish, meat; photos; map (M)

2000-11-18.title: Arafat Says He Has Told Gunmen to Stop Shooting
2000-11-18.abstract: Yasir Arafat says he has given orders for Palestinian gunmen in Palestinian-controlled territories to stop shooting; Prime Min Ehud Barak shows new flexibility as well by amending Israel's adamant stance that no international observers will be permitted to intervene in conflict; two steps represent a slight swiveling toward each other as Israelis and Palestinians work with Pres Clinton to try to find a way to revive the moribund peace effort; photo (M)

2000-11-19.title: In Not-So-Green Ireland, the Dispute Over Litter Gets Bitter
2000-11-19.abstract: Ireland is in the midst of a growing litter crisis, but political wrangling over how to change an antiquated waste management system is delaying any potential solution; Ireland puts 92 percent of its municipal waste into landfills, and recycling almost never happens in Irish households (M)

2000-11-19.title: Italian Sociologist's Goal: Make Opium Farming Fade Into History
2000-11-19.abstract: Pino Arlacchi, Italian sociologist and United Nations' top antinarcotics official, believes that Western nations can virtually eliminate opium poppy production in Afghanistan, Myanmar and Laos with a relatively small investment; says farmers do not get rich with opium production and are open to alternatives; photo; map (M)

2000-11-19.title: Mideast Lull Interrupted By Attack By Palestinian
2000-11-19.abstract: Jerusalem is free of explosive sounds of warfare for two days in row; Israeli military authorities caution against reading too much into lull, given seriousness of an early morning attack on Israeli Army post in Gaza by Palestinian security officer; Palestinans tell Israelis they will open investigation into incident, and ask them not to retaliate (M)

2000-11-19.title: Where Troops Fear to Tread, Aid Workers Are at Risk
2000-11-19.abstract: Killing of four staff members of United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees in West Timor in Sept 2000 prompts misgivings about sending aid workers to troubled places; more than 22,000 UN employees worldwide have asked Security Council to consider their security in situations of current or past conflict; Sec Gen Kofi Annan will seek $30 million a year from General Assembly to improve their security; 198 civilians working for UN have lost their lives since January 1992, and 240 have been kidnapped since January 1994; killers are almost never brought to justice (M)

2000-11-19.title: The Question of Liberty, And the Lure of America
2000-11-19.abstract: Young Vietnamese asked to comment on Pres Clinton's visit to their country express interest in America and civil liberties; Vietnamese newspapers focus on official face of Clinton's visit, not the rock-star reception he is receiving from people on the street (M)

2000-11-20.title: Sending Kosovars Home, an Awkward German Moment (and Don't Say Deport!)
2000-11-20.abstract: German repatriation of thousands of Kosovo Albanians who were given temporary refuge during war in Kosovo underscores intensifying debate about immigration in Germany, European country with largest population; debate centers on whether foreigners should be allowed to stay and, if so, how far they should adapt to local culture and on whether Germany, where nationhood has long been associated with blood lines and where it is more traditional to emigrate than to integrate strangers, should view itself of land of immigration at all; photo (M)

2000-11-20.title: Kawasaki City Journal; Forever Korean: Once Scorned, Always Scorned
2000-11-20.abstract: Koreans are emerging from status as Japan's largest but mostly silent and invisible minority, encouraged by election of Pres Kim Dae Jung in South Korea, who has long been advocate that they be given equal rights and the vote in Japan; issue has led to bitter divisions in Japan, forcing many people to re-examine long-held assumptions about what it means to be Japanese in ways that many find uncomfortable; being born in Japan does not automatically confer citizenship; non-Japanese can become naturalized and gain voting rights, but process is arduous and distasteful to many Koreans; photos (M)

2000-11-20.title: Peru's President To Resign Soon, Top Aides Say
2000-11-20.abstract: Alberto K Fujimori is expected to resign as president of Peru, ending tumultuous decade in power in which he battled terrorists, drug traffickers and hyperinflation before succumbing to series of scandals; Second Vice Pres Ricardo Marquez says he is prepared to take reins of power until new president can inaugurated on July 28; cabinet members tender resignations, but three most senior members say they will continue in posts until new transitional government is formed; Clinton administration appears surprised by move; photos (M)

2000-11-20.title: Hillary Clinton Sings Praises of Vietnam Women, and Vice Versa
2000-11-20.abstract: Hillary Rodham Clinton brings trademark mixture of feminism and family values to Vietnam, telling audience made up largely of women in Ho Chi Minh City that anything that is done to help women also helps children, families and society; pays homage to exalted place women occupy in Vietnamese history and their central role in country's commercial life; singles out Nguyen Thi Mai Thanh, who runs major enterprise; photo (M)

2000-11-20.title: India to Halt Military Acts in Kashmir for Muslim Holy Month
2000-11-20.abstract: Indian government, in rare, hopeful step toward ending more than decade of violence in Kashmir, says it will unilaterally halt military operations in troubled region of Kashmir during Muslim holy month of Ramadan (S)

2000-11-21.title: The New Burmese Leisure Class: Army Capitalists
2000-11-21.abstract: Rise of crony capitalism in ashes of failed socialism is new wrinkle to emerge in military dictatorship that has ruled Myanmar for four decades as generals throw off egalitarian shackles of Burmese socialism and are getting rich with their families and flaunting it; their conspicuous consumption is deeply offensive to Burmese traditions, and seems to celebrate widening gap between very rich and very poor; businessmen and Western diplomats say what is particularly crippling about situation is degree to which generals insist on sticking their fingers in every pie; affluent 'generals' village in downtown Mandalay, where military leaders have built gaudy new mansions, described; map; photo (M)

2000-11-21.title: Israel Charges Palestinians With Flouting Commitments
2000-11-21.abstract: Israel publishes detailed catalog of what it says is systematic Palestinian flouting of commitments under successive peace pacts and cease-fire accords; 48-page booklet, distributed to foreign governments and news organizations, accuses Palestinian Authority of wilfully exceeding negotiated limits on weapons stockpiles and uniform security personnel, while ignoring agreements on terrorism and protection of Jewish holy sites in its territory (M)

2000-11-21.title: Israel Strikes Back After a School Bus Is Bombed
2000-11-21.abstract: Powerful roadside bomb rips apart armored school bus filled with Israeli pupils in Gaza Strip, killing two adults and dismembering several children; heavy-caliber mortar shell encased in metal is detonated by remote control as army-escorted bus drove by; carnage described; Israel retaliates in most extensive air strikes yet; bombards military, police and media targets throughout Gaza; attack reportedly kills one Palestinian and critically wounds another; plunges much of Gaza City into darkness and sends ripples of panic throughout densely populated area; bus attack comes at end of three days of declining violence in West Bank and Gaza; succeeds in scuttling closed-door efforts on both sides to resume peace negotiations; Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat implores Arab leaders for support in war Israel has declared; Israel blames Arafat's Fatah organization; Palestinian Authority and Fatah deny any involvement in bombing; Palestinian government says it is opening investigation into attack and hints Islamic groups may be responsible; map; photos (M)

2000-11-21.title: After Gaza Rocket Attack, State Dept. Gives Israel Stern Warning
2000-11-21.abstract: New cycle of violence draws most severe warning to Israel by Clinton administration since fighting broke out seven weeks ago; State Department issues statement condemning attack against school bus filled with Israeli children in Gaza, but warns Israel excessive use of force in retaliation is not right way to go; notes 240 people have died in almost two months of violence, majority of them Palestinians (M)

2000-11-21.title: China Signs U.N. Pact on Rights and Rule of Law
2000-11-21.abstract: China signs UN agreement for cooperation and training on individual rights and rule of law; UN will advise country's police forces, courts and prisons on sound legal procedures; will monitor legal changes China must undertake to comply with two treaties it signed, but which have not been ratified, on economic and political rights; UN high commissioner for human rights, Mary Robinson, comments (M)

2000-11-22.title: Israeli Reprisals Leave Gaza Military Sites in Ruins
2000-11-22.abstract: Israeli reprisal attack in Gaza seems to have inflicted most of damage on designated targets, all symbols of Palestinian 'military establishment' Israel holds was complicit in bombing of school bus from Jewish settlement in Gaza Strip; none of damage seems crippling to police or political institutions involved; attack reportedly has deep psychological impact; Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak visits Gaza to assess damage (M)

2000-11-22.title: Egypt Recalls Envoy, Protesting Israeli Attack on Gaza
2000-11-22.abstract: Egypt recalls Amb Muhammad Bassiouny to protest Israel's bombardment of Gaza, which Arab world condemns as disproportionate response to Palestinian terrorist attack; move surprises Israeli officials, who hope to persuade Pres Hosni Mubarak to change his mind; Prime Min Ehud Barak cites importance of Egypt's role in Israeli-Palestinian peace process; Palestinian officials hail move as overdue; Barak says reprisal attack was also meant as warning to Palestinian Authority to halt violence; Israeli Itamar Yefet is shot to death in Gaza by Palestinian sharpshooter, angering Israeli settlers; photos (M)

2000-11-22.title: Mubarak's Move
2000-11-22.abstract: Analysis of Egyptian Pres Hosni Mubarak's decision to recall ambassador to protest Israel's reprisal attack on Gaza; move marks end of long period of relative restraint in Arab-Israeli ties; signals at least temporary end to chatty personal diplomacy that has become Mubarak's trademark (M)

2000-11-22.title: Checkpoint Sapper Journal; G.I.'s in Kosovo Are Judges, Jailers, and Much More
2000-11-22.abstract: United States and other NATO troops in Kosovo seek to keep tensions between Kosovo's Albanian majority and Serbian minority from spilling over to rest of Serbia; serve as part detective, part judge and part jailer; troops at checkpoint near Dobrosin, near province's border with rest of Serbia, comment; map; photo (M)

2000-11-22.title: Peru Congress Says Fujimori Is 'Unfit' and Picks Successor
2000-11-22.abstract: Peru's Congress, 62-9, declares Alberto K Fujimori 'morally unfit' for presidency, effectively leaving office vacant and sweeping aside his resignation; move clears way for Valentin Paniagua to succeed him, vaulting opposition to threshold of power for first time in over decade; some 30 congressmen allied with Fujimori storm out of session; Paniagua, head of small centrist party, seems unlikely to be more than caretaker president; photo (M)

2000-11-23.title: U.N. Envoy Voices Worry On U.S. Image
2000-11-23.abstract: Richard C Holbrooke, American ambassador to United Nations, voices fear that his yearlong campaign for lower dues for US could be stalled by nations that think no one is in charge in United States; Holbrooke has invited series of high-ranking members of Congress to UN to keep issue of budget reforms alive, and to counter impression that US government is frozen in crisis while presidential votes are being recounted in Florida (M)

2000-11-23.title: Russia Ending Deal on Arms Negotiated by Gore
2000-11-23.abstract: Russia notifies Clinton administration of its withdrawal from 1995 agreement negotiated by Vice Pres Al Gore limiting Russian sales of tanks and other conventional weapons to Iran; Pres Clinton protests Russian action during recent meeting with Pres Vladimir V Putin, and Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright warns Russian foreign minister, Igor S Ivanov, of economic sanctions against Russia if it makes new sales of advanced conventional weapons to Iran; at issue is confidential deal Gore reached with Viktor S Chernomyrdin, then Russian prime minister, to exempt Russia from sanctions for selling weapons to Iran in exchange for Moscow's pledge to end all deliveries of sophisticated conventional arms to Tehran by end of 1999; that deal seemingly undercut 1992 law sponsored by Gore and Sen John McCain that mandated sanctions against countries that sell such weapons to nations on State Dept list of state sponsors of terrorism; Iran is on list (M)

2000-11-23.title: Yemeni Cites Cole Suspects Seen as Linked to bin Laden
2000-11-23.abstract: Prime Min Abdel Karim al-Iryani of Yemen says investigation into attack on American destroyer Cole has identified bombers as two Saudi citizens with Yemeni family roots who fought Soviet forces in Afghanistan; two men have personal profiles so closely parallel to that of Osama bin Laden, whom Federal Bureau of Investigation is seeking on terror charges, that Yemeni investigators have concluded that bin Laden was at least indirectly involved in attack; so far they have no proof; photo (M)

2000-11-23.title: Protests Are Stepped Up at Hague Talks on Greenhouse Gases
2000-11-23.abstract: Protests reach high pitch in Hague as delegates from 175 countries continue to debate ways to slow emissions of heat-trapping greenhouse gases; demonstrators blockade doorways and top American negotiator, Frank E Loy, has cream pie thrown in his face; environmental groups that are lobbying delegations and observing discussions disavow attack, although they admit to being frustrated by slow pace of talks; Kyoto Protocol calls for industrialized nations to reduce their combined greenhouse-gas releases by 2012 to level at least 5 percent below emissions in 1990 (M)

2000-11-23.title: David Hermelin, 63, a Diplomat Who Artfully Used the Hot Dog
2000-11-23.abstract: David B Hermelin, former ambassador to Norway, real estate developer and philanthropist, dies at age 63; photo (M)

2000-11-24.title: Blair Criticizes Thatcher, Relegating Her to History
2000-11-24.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair attacks politics of Margaret Thatcher on 10th anniversary of the end of her prime ministry, saying they are irrelevant to contemporary Britain; says his government must deal today with the problems inherited from hers: boom-and-bust economics, social division and chronic underinvestment in public services; is partly provoked by Thatcher's criticism of Blair for agreeing to commit British troops to a new European rapid reaction force (M)

2000-11-24.title: Albright's Desire to Meet Yugoslav Leader Goes Unrequited
2000-11-24.abstract: New Serbian leader Vojislav Kostunica, who will attend annual session of foreign ministers of Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe in Vienna where he is expected to be star attraction, fails to respond to entreaties of American diplomats that he meet with Sec of State Madeleine Albright; image of both together could harm Kostunica at home since Albright is seen by many ordinary Serbs as embodiment of all ills that have befallen them from beyond their borders in last decade, culminating in NATO air strikes during war over Kosovo in 1999 (M)

2000-11-24.title: Dr. Louis Neel, 95, Physicist; Studied Aspects of Magnetism
2000-11-24.abstract: Dr Louis Neel, French physicist and Nobel laureate for his work in magnetic fields and their applications, dies at age 95; photo (M)

2000-11-24.title: To Germans' Surprise, Koreans Look to Them as 'Role Model' : Revisiting Lessons of Reunification
2000-11-24.abstract: DRESDEN—  For all its lingering flaws and costs, Germany's shotgun reunification 10 years ago has found fervent admirers on the other side of the globe.

2000-11-24.title: Time Is Short for U.S. to Join the International Criminal Court
2000-11-24.abstract: UN will begin two-week preparatory meeting on November 27 for establishing International Criminal Court, at which US is expected to announce final position on whether to sign treaty; 115 nations, including all of Europe and NATO governments, except US, are signatories (M)

2000-11-25.title: AIDS Infections Rise Globally, but Sub-Saharan Cases Stabilize
2000-11-25.abstract: World Health Organization reports 5.3 million people, including 600,000 children under age 15, became infected with AIDS virus during year; reports number of new infections in sub-Saharan Africa stabilized for first time, but was offset by increases in morbidity and deaths in region; estimated 3.8 million people in region were newly infected, down from 4 million in 1991; worldwide, estimated 36.1 million adults and children are infected--equally split between men and women (M)

2000-11-25.title: Theodore Monod, Sahara-Loving Naturalist, Dies at 98
2000-11-25.abstract: Theodore Andre Monod, distinguished French naturalist and authority on Sahara, dies at age 98; photo (M)

2000-11-25.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-11-25.abstract: THE AMERICAS    PERU: INVESTIGATION OF FUJIMORI  A special investigator said he had asked the attorney general's office to start a criminal investigation of former President Alberto Fujimori, left, for possible corruption. Jose Ugaz, whom Mr. Fujimori himself appointed to investigate his former intelligence chief, Vladimiro Montesinos, said the request covered a range of possible crimes, including money laundering, corruption of public officials and illicit enrichment. Mr. Fujimori resigned as president Monday while in Japan and said he plans to stay in his ancestral homeland.   (AP)    ARGENTINA: STRIKES AGAINST AUSTERITY  Millions of workers stayed off their jobs in the largest national strike in years as a union-led protest against government austerity measures virtually paralyzed the country. One person was killed as protesters blocked highways with flaming tires and forced schools, businesses and transportation services to shut down. The strike even hit Argentina's national passion, causing the postponement of soccer matches.    (AP)

2000-11-25.title: For Mainlanders, Taiwan's Lure Proves Powerful but Often Elusive
2000-11-25.abstract: Mainland Chinese being detained in Taiwan as illegal immigrants reinforce impression that the vibrant, economically advanced island is seen in China more as a model than a pariah; an estimated half of the 80,000 mainlanders living in Taiwan are there illegally, and some officials regard them as a potential threat to national security in event of a war; China is often inexplicably slow in deciding to recognize identities of the illegal immigrants and permit their return to mainland, where they may face fines but no drastic punishment; 373 men and 245 women are held at camp south of Taipei on a recent day, leading a regimented and rather spartan existence, but by no means a brutal one, behind barred doors and windows and barbed wire; photos (M)

2000-11-25.title: Agrigento Journal; In Juno's Shadow, Temples of Illegal Construction
2000-11-25.abstract: Sprawl of apartment buildings, cottages and villas flourishes across most of Italy, but most notably in Agrigento section of Sicily at perimeter of ruins of Greek temples that date from fifth century BC in illegal construction phenomenon called abusivismo; buildings were put up by abusivi, as owners are known, during postwar boom when even most flagrant zoning abuses were ignored by elected officials and city planners; practice, which is deeply entrenched political and cultural phenomenon all over Sicily, illustrates difficulties of bringing order and prosperity to country's south; buildings have become glaring urban blight as well as safety hazard; maps; photo (M)

2000-11-26.title: TREATY TALKS FAIL TO FIND CONSENSUS IN GLOBAL WARMING
2000-11-26.abstract: Last-minute disputes between European and American negotiators result in collapse of negotiations aimed at finishing treaty to curb global warming; breakdown follows two weeks of intensive talks in Hague, stunning many participants, environmental groups and observers; Frank E Loy, top American negotiator, agrees with several European participants that effort should continue, even when he and Clinton administration hand over task to new administration; talks are riven by conflicting agendas; poor countries seek billions of dollars to help them adapt to climate change, and rich nations seek to blunt economic impact of treaty by finding least costly ways to cut their emissions of warming gases; latest failure attributed to persistent disagreement between industrial powers on opposite sides of Atlantic over role of trees and properly managed farmland in acting as 'sinks' to absorb carbon dioxide, dominant greenhouse gas; photo (M)

2000-11-26.title: Irish Unions, Pay Lagging, Stage Protests
2000-11-26.abstract: Irish labor unions, after more than decade of cordial industrial relations, return to picket lines with series of intermittent one-day strikes by workers on railroads and in hospitals, by airline pilots and journalists; unions contend that rising prices--Ireland's inflation rate is currently nearly 7 percent, more than double European average--have made 5.5 percent wage increases meaningless; sporadic unrest orchestrated this month by 16,000 high school teachers is considered by some experts to pose most serious threat so far to Ireland's 13-year-old 'social partnership' of industrial relations (M)

2000-11-26.title: In Philippines, The President Could Evade An Ouster Yet
2000-11-26.abstract: Pres Joseph Estrada of Philippines, who has been impeached by House of Representatives, is being deserted by leading allies and members of his cabinet as Senate prepares to begin trial that could result in his removal; but Estrada has not caved in as opponents had hoped, and political experts say battle could last for weeks or months (M)

2000-11-26.title: New Religious Clash on Indonesian Island
2000-11-26.abstract: JAKARTA, Indonesia, Nov. 25 — At least 10 people, most Christians, have been killed and 16 are missing in the latest fighting between Christians and Muslims in the Moluccas, the police said today.

2000-11-26.title: Haiti Sees A Candidate With a Pair Of Visions
2000-11-26.abstract: Former Haitian Pres Jean-Bertrand Aristide is only real candidate in Nov 26 presidential election, because all opposition political parties, civic groups and international observers are sitting out contest after flawed vote-counting in legislative elections in May gave Aristide's Lavalas party an overwhelming majority in Parliament (M)

2000-11-27.title: Romanian Ex-President Winning, but Runoff Is Expected
2000-11-27.abstract: Former Pres Ion Iliescu seems headed for victory in Romania's presidential election, but he will face runoff against nationalist extremist Corneliu Vadim Tudor, who has soared in opinion polls in past month; photos (M)

2000-11-27.title: Change Bewilders South Africa's White Voters
2000-11-27.abstract: Growing number of South Africa's whites are confused, disillusioned and undecided over what party to support as country prepares to vote on December 5 for local council members in 284 cities and towns; there is lack of faith or trust in all white opposition Democratic Alliance, which many accuse of behaving like African National Congress and which has started courting black vote; feeling among Afrikaners in Sophiatown, neighborhood built on ruins of storied black community that was forcibly removed in 1950's to make room for working-class Afrikaners, discussed; photos (M)

2000-11-27.title: Haiti Casts Ballots, Preparing the Way For Aristide Encore
2000-11-27.abstract: Voter turnout is light in Haiti in election seen likely to be won by former Pres Jean-Bertrand Aristide, who faced no serious challengers after opposition announced boycott; election poses quandry for international community and US, which have expressed grave reservations about legitimacy of both vote and fairness of May legislative election which gave Aristide's party overwhelming majority in Parliament; photos (M)

2000-11-27.title: Canadians Voting Today in a Close Race
2000-11-27.abstract: Canadians will vote on November 27 for new government after five-week campaign whose outcome opinion polls suggest is too close to call with approximately 20 percent of voters undecided among candidates from five parties; race could decide political fate of Prime Min Jean Chretien, who has headed government for past seven years, and faces stiff opposition from principal challenger Stockwell Day, conservative who heads new Canadian Alliance; polls indicate Liberals will lose seats in West to Alliance and to separatist Parti Quebecois in Quebec and Ontario; map; photos (M)

2000-11-27.title: Thais Will Not Vote Until January, but the Small Bills and Gifts Are Already Flying
2000-11-27.abstract: Parliamentary election campaign begins in Thailand; it is first in which vote for lower house is being held under new reformist Constitution that created Election Commission empowered to disqualify candidates caught cheating; even so, officials estimate that by voting day on January 6, candidates will have spent as much as $460 million to buy votes, much of it in small bills and gifts (M)

2000-11-28.title: Citizen Fujimori, Starting Over Again
2000-11-28.abstract: Interview with former Peruvian Pres Alberto K Fujimori, who is adjusting to new life as private citizen in Tokyo, Japan; defends his past; says he has no regrets about fateful decisions her made during his decade of rule in Peru; photo (M)

2000-11-28.title: Leader of Tamil Tiger Rebels Says He Is Ready for Peace Talks
2000-11-28.abstract: Velupillai Prabhakaran, leader of separatist Tamil Tiger rebels in Sri Lanka and one of world's most ruthless guerrilla strategists, says group is ready for unconditional peace talks with the Sri Lankan government to end 17-year-old ethnic conflict; calls on government to create climate of good will by ending economic blockade of rebel-controlled parets of Sri Lanka and de-escalating war; overture follows Norwegian mediation in conflict between rebels from mostly Hindu Tamil minority and Buddhist Sinhalese-dominated government (M)

2000-11-28.title: American in Moscow Spy Trial Claims Victory on Key Evidence
2000-11-28.abstract: Pavel Astakhov, lawyer for American businessman Edmond Pope, who has been charged with espionage in Russia, says judge hearing case has admitted crucial piece of evidence that could help exonerate him; says defense will be able to submit documents showing Pope and Russian professor Anatoly Babkin were trading unclassified information about torpedo technology when arrested in April (S)

2000-11-28.title: Yugoslav Faults West Over Attacks on Serbs
2000-11-28.abstract: Yugoslav Pres Vojislav Kostunica, warning that violence spilling over from Kosovo could easily set entire region ablaze, accuses UN and Western powers of failing to do their part to stop armed Albanian attacks on Serbian police and citizens; addresses annual gathering of Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe, Vienna, where he presses for stronger action by NATO-led forces that control Kosovo against Albanian fighters operating inside rest of Serbia; foreign ministers welcome Kostunica and Yugoslavia back into organization; he snubs Sec of State Madeleine K Albright, one of main advocates of NATO's war against Yugoslavia, also attending conference; photo (M)

2000-11-28.title: Big Shoes to Fill, as Welch Picks Successor at GE
2000-11-28.abstract: NEW YORK—  Beginning a year-long transition, General Electric Co. announced Monday that Jeffrey Immelt had won a three-way race to succeed Jack Welch as chairman of the world's most valuable company.

2000-11-29.title: Tally in Romania Is Official: Crucial Runoff Is Due Dec. 10
2000-11-29.abstract: Nearly complete vote tally in Romania gives former Pres Ion Iliescu, one-time Communist official, 37 percent of presidential vote to 28 percent for nationalist extremist Corneliu Vadim Tudor; two will face each other in Dec 10 runoff; Iliescu, whose party is expected to have 47 percent of Parliament seats, will have problems forming government because he critized other parties from centrist coalition heavily during campaign; experts are unsure he will win runoff (M)i

2000-11-29.title: ASEAN Warns EU to Ease Its Burma Boycott
2000-11-29.abstract: SINGAPORE—  Southeast Asian countries have told the European Union they will abandon high-level talks with the EU if it continues to press for Burma's exclusion on human rights grounds.

2000-11-29.title: India's Truce Over Kashmir Is Shattered As 3 More Die
2000-11-29.abstract: India's unilateral cease-fire, peace offering to militant groups waging war in Kashmir in honoor of Islamic holy month, lasts some 14 hours until booby trap blows up army vehicle, killing three soldiers and wounding dozen; militant group Hizbul Mujahedeen claims responsibility; map (M)

2000-11-29.title: Shareholders Revolt as Automaker Stumbles : Daimler Chief Under Fire
2000-11-29.abstract: FRANKFURT—  Juergen Schrempp, the swaggering chairman of DaimlerChrysler AG, is battling an increasingly militant shareholder revolt that threatens to toss him from his job.

2000-11-30.title: With Economy Brighter, Brazil's Leader Faces Wage Demands
2000-11-30.abstract: Brazil's economy rebounds, two years after country signed $41.5 billion emergency rescue package with international lending agencies, but salaries continue to lag; Pres Fernando Henrique Cardoso is facing demands that gap be closed with big increases in minimum wage and military pay, and Congress is poised to approve legislation that would raise minimum wage by 19 percent, to $92.31 per month; Cardoso endorses legislation but warns granting increase to government employees will require about $2 billion in spending cuts or additional taxes because of requirements of new fiscal responsibility law that is part of economic recovery program (M)

2000-11-30.title: France's Top Leaders at Odds Over Presidential Election Timing
2000-11-30.abstract: France's Socialist Prime Min Lionel Jospin and archrival Gaullist Pres Jacques Chirac publicly squabble over timing of presidential election after Jospin issues unexpected call to move 2002 vote ahead of legislative contests, possibly undermining Chirac's re-election strategy; Chirac, who opposes earlier election, hopes current system will allow conservative parties to win majority in Parliament at polls in March 2002, making it harder for Jospin to win presidency two months later; earlier presidential election would focus race squarely on rivalry between two men (M)

2000-11-30.title: South Africa to Ask for International Gem Certification
2000-11-30.abstract: South Africa will introduce resolution in UN General Assembly for international certification system for gems, amid concern that diamonds may not only be fueling civil wars among other Africans but also adverse publicity and calls for boycotts that will hurt its own diamond-mining industry (M)

2000-11-30.title: Horrors Behind Rebel Lines in Sierra Leone
2000-11-30.abstract: Rebels of Revolutionary United Front in Sierra Leone have deliberately made civilians their targets, killing, raping and maiming them; horrors are revealed in Bumbuna, small but fertile village in rebel-held territory, whose people have been prevented from farming and have become shrunken and shriveled; photos; maps (M)

2000-11-30.title: Greenland Is Pampered But Ponders Cutting Free
2000-11-30.abstract: Greenland seems resigned to remain Home Rule colony of Denmark, despite pressures for independence; while world's largest island is geologically part of North America, lying only 200 miles east of Canada, it overwhelmingly depends on Europe; Denmark subsidizes it with $460 million per year, or about $8,300 for each of its 55,000 inhabitants; has established Scandinavian-style living standards on island rarely seen in Arctic, after weaning Greenland from American military control during World War II (M)
